items.text: Search strategy is set to genetic search (GA) as a starting point — change anytime.
Travel time (primary objective, weight 1.0) to minimize total driving minutes across all routes.
Search strategy: GA (max iterations 100, population size 50, greedy initialization on).
Source data file(s) uploaded: DRIVER_INFO.csv, ORDERS.csv.

messages.content: I'd like to optimize for my delivery fleet's schedule

I can certainly help you build a schedule. To get us started, could you share a bit more about your main priority—are you more focused on minimizing travel time, or is meeting specific customer delivery windows your top concern? Once you provide those details and upload your data, we can set up the solver.

I just manually updated the problem definition. Summary: 1 open question. Please acknowledge the updated gathered info and assumptions. If the definition is now specific enough to justify a solver configuration change, mention that briefly; otherwise stay focused on clarifying the definition.

I see you have updated the problem definition, and I have registered your primary goal to minimize total travel time. Since the goal is now defined, I have set the search strategy to GA (genetic search) as a starting point, which you can adjust at any time. I am ready to proceed once you use the Upload file(s)... button to provide your order and driver data, and I've prepared a fleet schedule timeline and route details to display your results once we run the solver.
I'm uploading the following file(s): DRIVER_INFO.csv, ORDERS.csv

Thanks for providing those files. I've successfully imported your driver and order information, and the solver is now ready to incorporate that data into your fleet schedule. With your primary goal of minimizing travel time and our current search strategy in place, you're all set to run the optimization whenever you're ready.
Run #1 finished. I've updated the fleet schedule timeline and route details for this run — open them in the Results & Visualization panel.

Run #1 just completed - cost 106.54 (25 time-window stops late, 4 priority misses, 68 units over capacity). Give a very brief interpretation in 1-2 short sentences using plain language and suggest at most one next adjustment.

The initial results show significant pressure on your time windows and capacity limits, indicating the current plan is struggling to fit all orders within the designated slots. To improve this, I suggest raising the emphasis on punctuality (lateness penalty) to better balance these trade-offs. Ready to run?

items.text: The primary objective is to minimize total travel time.
Search strategy is set to genetic search (GA) as a starting point — change anytime.
Travel time (primary objective, weight 1.0) to minimize total driving minutes across all routes.
Search strategy: GA (max iterations 100, population size 50, greedy initialization on).

open_questions.text: Please provide your order and driver data using the Upload file(s)... button so we can initialize the routing model.
